title: Stay Hydrated
summary: Recognize signs of dehydration and take steps to drink enough water.
learnerPromise: You will be able to notice when your body needs more fluids and build a simple habit of drinking water throughout the day.
whyItMatters: Staying hydrated supports your energy, focus, and body temperature. Many people do not drink enough water, especially during warm weather or physical activity.
realLifeUses: Drinking enough water before and during sports practice or exercise
Noticing a headache or dry mouth as a signal to drink water
Keeping a water bottle nearby during a long school or work day
Helping a friend remember to hydrate on a hot outdoor outing
materialsNeeded: Optional: phone alarm or hydration tracking app
result: A personal hydration plan with at least two reminders or cues built into your daily routine.
youWillLearn: Recognize common early signs that your body needs more fluids
Estimate how much water you may need based on your activity level
Build simple cues and reminders into your daily routine
Choose water-rich foods and drinks as backup hydration sources
miniChallenge: Drink a full glass of water right now and set two reminders on your phone to drink water later today.
steps: Notice how you feel right now: tired, headache, dry mouth, or dark urine can be signs you need water.
Fill a bottle or glass and drink a full serving.
Decide on two daily cues for drinking water, such as with meals or when you wake up.
Set reminders on your phone or place your bottle where you will see it often.
Check in with yourself later today to see if the reminders helped.
completionCriteria: You drink at least one full glass or bottle of water during this skill
You have at least two reminders or cues set for the rest of the day
You can name three early signs of dehydration
commonProblems: Forgetting to drink water when busy or focused on other tasks
Not feeling thirsty even when your body needs fluids
Preferring sugary drinks over water, which can reduce how much you drink
tips: Keep a filled water bottle in your bag, on your desk, or by your bed
Drink a glass of water with each meal as a built-in habit
Add a slice of lemon or cucumber if plain water feels boring to you
helpBoundary: If you show signs of severe dehydration such as dizziness, very dark urine, rapid heartbeat, or confusion, contact a medical professional or urgent care.
